page: OVHcloud r3-32 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs, utilizing AMD EPYC Milan processors running at 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 100 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance is provisioned with a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 3077.24 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1910.0, both placing in the top 25%. Multi-core performance is average, yielding a Geekbench multi-core score of 6080.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 12261.5. Memory bandwidth tests show average throughput, reaching 120.0 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 74.19 GB/sec for large block write operations.

The server exhibits certain performance limitations, particularly in static web serving throughput, which recorded a weak score of 4.42 GB/sec. Additionally, prompt processing for medium-sized (7B) LLM models is weak at 21.45 tokens/sec, though text generation for the same model remains average at 13.58 tokens/sec. Given the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and average database operation benchmarks, this instance is configured for memory-intensive workloads, caching layers such as Redis, and moderate database operations.
description: OVHcloud r3-32 is a memory-optimized virtual server instance built on the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC Milan processors operating at 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.0 GB per core, alongside a bundled 100 GB NVMe SSD and a 1 Gbps baseline network interface. Benchmark data highlights strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1910.0, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance. However, the instance demonstrates weak static web serving throughput at 4.42 GB/sec. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and solid Redis benchmark of 9.91 million SET operations per second, this server is suited for memory-intensive applications, caching layers, and database workloads.
og_description: OVHcloud r3-32 is a memory-optimized KVM instance featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and a 100 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud r3-32 is a memory-optimized KVM server with 4 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 100 GB NVMe SSD, built for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, balanced memory density, and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
Bundled 100 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Suited for caching layers, database operations, and memory-heavy tasks
